## reference · WCAG Contrast Ratio 4.5 Accessibility
* The visual presentation of text and images of text must have a contrast ratio of at least **4.5:1** (Level AA).
* Large text (18pt or larger, or 14pt and larger if bold) has a contrast ratio requirement of at least **3:1**.
* Incidental text (inactive UI components, pure decoration, not visible to anyone, or part of a picture with significant other visual content) has **no contrast requirement**.
* Logotypes have **no contrast requirement**.
* Text over gradients, semi-transparent colors, and background images must meet contrast requirements; test the area where contrast is lowest.
* Color changes on hover, focus, etc. must meet the same contrast requirements and be evaluated independently.
* Non-text elements (user interface components and graphical objects) must have a contrast ratio of at least **3:1** against adjacent colors.
* Exceptions for non-text elements: inactive components, or where the appearance is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author.

Sources: https://webaim.org/articles/contrast/
